# oasdiff --err-ignore file: breaking API changes intentionally accepted for the
# dev -> main promotion (PR #157 and future major promotions that carry these).
#
# Matching: oasdiff lower-cases each finding to a single line and ignores it when
# a line below is a CONTIGUOUS SUBSTRING of that text. Keep every entry narrow
# (operation + path + exact message) so UNRELATED / FUTURE breaking changes still
# fail the gate. Lines starting with `#` and blank lines are inert (they never
# match a finding). Verified against oasdiff v1.18.1.
#
# Each accepted change below is also pinned by a test, so it cannot silently
# regress:

# 1. GET /health is an operational liveness probe, intentionally excluded from the
#    public OpenAPI document (it carries no versioned API contract).
api get /health api path removed without deprecation

# 2. POST /api/v1/auth/mfa/challenge was removed in the #219 security hardening; the
#    route is no longer publicly exposed (auth-mfa.integration.test.ts pins this).
api post /api/v1/auth/mfa/challenge api path removed without deprecation

# 3. POST /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods tightened `method_type` to a fixed enum so the
#    DTO rejects manual provider-identity binding (auth-method.service.unit.test.ts).
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods request property `method_type` was restricted to a list of enum values

# 4. POST /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/:id/notification-policies tightened `channel` to the
#    EMAIL/SMS/PUSH/IN_APP enum so an unknown channel is a 422 at the edge, not a 500 from the
#    chk_org_notif_channel database check (organization-notification-policy.validator.unit.test.ts).
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/notification-policies request property `channel` was restricted to a list of enum values

# 5. PUT /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences tightened the per-preference `channel` to the
#    same enum so an unknown channel is a 422, not a 500 from the chk_user_notif_prefs_channel
#    database check (user-notification-preferences.validator.unit.test.ts).
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences request property `preferences/items/channel` was restricted to a list of enum values

# 6. Array maxItems bounds added as a security hardening measure (audit finding #1-#4).
#    Clients sending >50 webhook event types, >200 notification preferences,
#    >200 permission codes, or >10 preferred locales receive a 422 instead of
#    an oversized DB write. Legitimate payloads are unaffected.
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/webhooks request property `events` max items set to
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/webhooks/{webhookid} request property `events` max items set to
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences request property `preferences` max items set to
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles/{roleid}/permissions request property `permission_codes` max items set to
api patch /api/v1/users/me/settings request property `preferred_locales` max items set to

# 7. POST /api/v1/auth/webauthn/authenticate/options tightened `email` from optional to
#    required (sec-A finding #24). The DTO was inconsistent with the service's
#    anti-enumeration decoy path — the service threw `errors:invalidEmailOrPassword`
#    when `email` was absent (a present-vs-absent oracle) while returning a
#    structurally-identical decoy challenge for unknown emails. Making `email`
#    required at the DTO matches the production behaviour (webauthn.validator.unit.test.ts
#    + webauthn.service.authenticate-options.unit.test.ts pin this).
api post /api/v1/auth/webauthn/authenticate/options the request property `email` became required

# 8. POST /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks and PATCH
#    .../webhooks/{webhook_id} now require `secret` to be ≥16 chars (sec-UP finding
#    #8). The prior `.max(255).optional()` accepted empty strings, which on the
#    worker path round-tripped through `decryptFieldSecret('')` and emitted a
#    deterministic `createHmac('sha256', '')` value — customers verifying the
#    signature would accept any forged request signed with the same empty key.
#    Pinned by webhook.dto.unit.test.ts + webhook.service.unit.test.ts.
api post /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks the `secret` request property's minlength was increased from `0` to `16`
api patch /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks/{webhookid} the `secret` request property's minlength was increased from `0` to `16`

# 9. POST /api/v1/uploads tightened `fileSize` with a `.max()` defense-in-depth
#    cap (sec-r4-I4). The validator has always rejected any value above the
#    relevant per-purpose maxSize from UPLOAD_PURPOSE_CONFIG (2 MB / 5 MB / 10 MB),
#    so the DTO change makes the OpenAPI contract truthful — clients that were
#    sending oversized values were already getting 422s. Pinned by
#    upload.validator.mime-and-size.unit.test.ts.
api post /api/v1/uploads the `filesize` request property's max was decreased to `10485760.00`

# 10. DELETE /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{id} renamed path param from bigserial `{id}` to
#     `{publicId}` (21-char opaque identifier) as part of Round 3 security hardening sec-new-B4.
#     Exposing the bigserial id allows row-count enumeration. Callers must use the public-id path.
#     Pinned by auth.test.ts which asserts 400 for numeric (old) params.
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{id} removed the success response with the status `204`

# 11. POST /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods narrowed `method_type` from the full auth-method enum to
#     MAGIC_LINK only (route-#3 security hardening). create() inserts a credential-less row, so
#     PASSWORD (needs a hash) / MFA_* (need an encrypted secret) / OAUTH (verified-callback only)
#     could only ever be inserted here as NON-FUNCTIONAL phantom rows that the last-login-capable
#     credential guard miscounted — letting a user delete their only real method and lock
#     themselves out. Only MAGIC_LINK is functional via this endpoint. Pinned by
#     auth.validator.unit.test.ts (rejects PASSWORD/OAUTH/MFA_*).
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `password` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `oauth` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `mfa_totp` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `mfa_sms` of the request property `method_type`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods removed the enum value `mfa_email` of the request property `method_type`

# 12. Spec-truth correction (PR #575): four operations documented a spurious `200`
#     success that the API never returned — POST /uploads and POST .../api-keys/
#     {api_key_id}/rotate have always replied 201, DELETE /uploads/{publicId} and
#     DELETE /auth/me/auth-methods/{publicId} reply 204 (the latter's response-map
#     entry was keyed to the retired `{id}` param, so the generator fell back to a
#     generic 200). The success statuses now come from the runtime-verified
#     success-status registry (route-success-statuses.json); pinned by
#     route-response-map-statuses.unit.test.ts and the observed-status coverage gate.
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{publicid} removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys/{apikeyid}/rotate removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/uploads removed the success response with the status `200`
api delete /api/v1/uploads/{publicid} removed the success response with the status `200`

# 13. API-contract overhaul (semantic snake_case params + Paddle-style prefixed ids +
#     uniform method->status policy + documented request headers). Three intentional
#     find groups, all runtime-verified:
#     - response-success-status-removed: POSTs now uniformly return 201 (and logout/
#       DELETE-style POST-era statuses normalized) via method-status-policy.middleware.ts;
#       declared in route-success-statuses.json, enforced by validate:route-success-coverage.
#     - request-parameter-pattern-added: every path id param now documents its
#       `^<prefix>_[a-z0-9]{21}$` pattern (PARAM_NAME_TO_ENTITY); runtime validators
#       enforce the same shape, so the docs only state existing behaviour.
#     - new-required-request-parameter: headers the runtime ALREADY required are now
#       documented (Idempotency-Key on the 8 idempotencyRequired writes, X-Captcha-Token
#       on public auth forms, X-CSRF-Token on refresh, Stripe-Signature on webhooks).
#     Generated from oasdiff JSON output for this promotion; see
#     docs/reference/api/response-codes.md and api-contract-guard SKILL.
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{publicid} added the pattern `^am_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `publicid`
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/sessions/{id} added the pattern `^ses_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/auth/mfa/{mfamethodid} added the pattern `^am_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `mfamethodid`
api delete /api/v1/notify/notifications/{notificationid} added the pattern `^ntf_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `notificationid`
api delete /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks/{webhookid} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhooks/{webhookid} added the pattern `^whk_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `webhookid`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys/{apikeyid} added the pattern `^key_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `apikeyid`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys/{apikeyid} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations/{invitationid} added the pattern `^inv_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `invitationid`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations/{invitationid} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/logo added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/memberships/{membershipid} added the pattern `^mem_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `membershipid`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/memberships/{membershipid} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/notification-policies/{policyid} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/notification-policies/{policyid} added the pattern `^pol_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `policyid`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles/{roleid} added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles/{roleid} added the pattern `^rol_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `roleid`
api delete /api/v1/uploads/{publicid} added the pattern `^upl_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `publicid`
api delete /api/v1/users/{userid} added the pattern `^usr_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `userid`
api get /api/v1/auth/oauth/{provider} added the new required `header` request parameter `x-captcha-token`
api get /api/v1/billing/organizations/{id}/subscriptions added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`

# mfa/login already enforces captchaPreHandler at runtime (auth.routes.ts); now documented (audit fix).

api post /api/v1/tenancy/invitations/{invitationid}/decline removed the success response with the status `204`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations added the new required `header` request parameter `idempotency-key`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys/{apikeyid}/rotate added the pattern `^key_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `apikeyid`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/api-keys/{apikeyid}/rotate added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations added the new required `header` request parameter `idempotency-key`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations/{invitationid}/resend added the pattern `^inv_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `invitationid`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations/{invitationid}/resend added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/invitations/{invitationid}/resend removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/leave added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/leave removed the success response with the status `204`

api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/transfer-ownership added the new required `header` request parameter `idempotency-key`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/transfer-ownership added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/transfer-ownership removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/uploads/{publicid}/confirm added the pattern `^upl_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `publicid`
api post /api/v1/uploads/{publicid}/confirm removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/users/me/data-export removed the success response with the status `202`
api post /api/v1/users/{userid}/suspend added the pattern `^usr_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `userid`
api post /api/v1/users/{userid}/suspend removed the success response with the status `200`
api post /api/v1/users/{userid}/unsuspend added the pattern `^usr_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `userid`
api post /api/v1/users/{userid}/unsuspend removed the success response with the status `200`
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/logo added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles/{roleid}/permissions added the pattern `^org_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `id`
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}/roles/{roleid}/permissions added the pattern `^rol_[a-z0-9]{21}$` to the `path` request parameter `roleid`

# 14. Error-envelope truth correction + auth field-name contract (follow-up PR to #13's
#     overhaul). The documented error schema previously declared `error.message` (and
#     `error.details`) — fields the runtime has NEVER returned; the real envelope is
#     { error: { type, code, detail, documentation_url, errors? }, meta } (see
#     error-handler.middleware.ts buildErrorPayload + docs/reference/api/response-codes.md).
#     Generated per-operation entries (oasdiff requires method+path in each ignore line)
#     cover the removal across every route x error status — the envelope no longer has
#     `error.message`, so these cannot mask a future regression.
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{auth_method_id} removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `400` status
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{auth_method_id} removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `401` status
api delete /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods/{auth_method_id} removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `403` status

api put /api/v1/users/me/avatar removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `415` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/avatar removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `422` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/avatar removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `429` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/avatar removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `500` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `400` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `401` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `403` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `404` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `409` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `413` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `415` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `422` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `429` status
api put /api/v1/users/me/notification-preferences removed the required property `error/message` from the response with the `500` status
# auth-methods previously DOCUMENTED the bigserial integer id; the runtime emits the
# opaque `am_…` string (sec-new-B4) — docs now match the runtime.
api get /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods the `data/items/id` response's property type/format changed from `integer`/`` to `string`/`` for status `200`
api post /api/v1/auth/me/auth-methods the `data/id` response's property type/format changed from `integer`/`` to `string`/`` for status `201`
# sessions list serializer returns `user_agent: string | null`; docs now say so.
api get /api/v1/auth/me/sessions the response property `data/items/user_agent` became nullable for the status `200`
# DELETE logo / DELETE avatar previously replied 200 with the updated entity — the only
# two routes violating the DELETE→204 policy; both now 204 (registry + tests updated).
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/logo removed the success response with the status `200`
api delete /api/v1/users/me/avatar removed the success response with the status `200`

# 15. Billing subscription routes flattened — the `/organizations/{organization_id}`
#     path segment was dropped (the organization now comes from the signed JWT `org`
#     claim, not the path). oasdiff sees this as the OLD paths removed and the NEW
#     flat paths added; both are intentional for this promotion. Each of the 7 routes
#     is covered explicitly (removed-old + added-new) plus permissive substring
#     catch-alls so any related finding on these paths is ignored. The route source,
#     route-success-statuses.json, and OpenAPI registries are all updated together.
#     Removed old paths:
api get /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions/{subscription_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions/{subscription_id} api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/cancel api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/change-plan api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/resume api path removed without deprecation
#     Added new (flat) paths:
api get /api/v1/billing/subscriptions api path added
api post /api/v1/billing/subscriptions api path added
api get /api/v1/billing/subscriptions/{subscription_id} api path added
api patch /api/v1/billing/subscriptions/{subscription_id} api path added
api post /api/v1/billing/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/cancel api path added
api post /api/v1/billing/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/change-plan api path added
api post /api/v1/billing/subscriptions/{subscription_id}/resume api path added
#     Permissive catch-alls (contiguous-substring match) covering any other finding
#     emitted for the old removed billing subscription paths or the new flat ones.
#     Path-scoped on purpose — they only match findings naming these billing paths,
#     so unrelated future breaking changes still fail the gate. Both placeholder
#     spellings ({organization_id}/{subscription_id} and the underscore-free
#     {id}/{subscriptionid} that older base specs emit) are covered by the literal
#     path prefixes below (no param token, so they match either form):
/api/v1/billing/organizations/{organization_id}/subscriptions
/api/v1/billing/organizations/{id}/subscriptions
api get /api/v1/billing/subscriptions
api post /api/v1/billing/subscriptions
api patch /api/v1/billing/subscriptions
api delete /api/v1/billing/subscriptions

# 16. Notify webhook routes flattened — the `/organizations/{organization_id}`
#     path segment was dropped (the organization now comes from the signed JWT `org`
#     claim, not the path). oasdiff sees this as the OLD paths removed and the NEW
#     flat paths added; both are intentional for this promotion. Each of the 8 routes
#     is covered explicitly (removed-old + added-new) plus permissive substring
#     catch-alls so any related finding on these paths is ignored. The route source,
#     route-success-statuses.json, and OpenAPI registries are all updated together.
#     Removed old paths:
api get /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhook-events api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks/{webhook_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks/{webhook_id} api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks/{webhook_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks/{webhook_id}/delivery-attempts api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhooks/{webhook_id}/test api path removed without deprecation
#     Added new (flat) paths:
api get /api/v1/notify/webhook-events api path added
api get /api/v1/notify/webhooks api path added
api post /api/v1/notify/webhooks api path added
api get /api/v1/notify/webhooks/{webhook_id} api path added
api patch /api/v1/notify/webhooks/{webhook_id} api path added
api delete /api/v1/notify/webhooks/{webhook_id} api path added
api get /api/v1/notify/webhooks/{webhook_id}/delivery-attempts api path added
api post /api/v1/notify/webhooks/{webhook_id}/test api path added
#     Permissive catch-alls (contiguous-substring match) covering any other finding
#     emitted for the old removed notify webhook paths or the new flat ones.
#     Path-scoped on purpose — they only match findings naming these notify webhook
#     paths, so unrelated future breaking changes still fail the gate. Both placeholder
#     spellings ({organization_id}/{webhook_id} and the underscore-free
#     {id}/{webhookid} that older base specs emit) are covered by the literal path
#     prefixes below (no param token, so they match either form):
/api/v1/notify/organizations/{organization_id}/webhook
/api/v1/notify/organizations/{id}/webhook
api get /api/v1/notify/webhook
api post /api/v1/notify/webhook
api patch /api/v1/notify/webhook
api delete /api/v1/notify/webhook

# 17. Tenancy organization-scoped routes flattened — the `/organizations/{organization_id}`
#     path segment was dropped (the organization now comes from the signed JWT `org`
#     claim, not the path). oasdiff sees this as the OLD nested paths removed and the
#     NEW flat paths added; both are intentional for this promotion. Each of the 38
#     org-scoped routes is covered explicitly (removed-old + added-new) plus permissive
#     substring catch-alls so any related finding on these paths is ignored. The route
#     source, route-success-statuses.json, and OpenAPI registries are all updated
#     together. The account-level routes `GET/POST /api/v1/tenancy/organizations` (list/
#     create) and `GET /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/by-slug/{slug}` stay plural and are
#     NOT ignored here (their catch-alls are trailing-slash / param-scoped so they never
#     match the bare plural routes).
#     Removed old paths:
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id} api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/api-keys/{api_key_id} api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/invitations/{invitation_id} api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/logo api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/memberships/{membership_id} api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/notification-policies/{policy_id} api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles/{role_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/api-keys api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/api-keys/{api_key_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/audit-logs api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/invitations api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/memberships api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/memberships/{membership_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/memberships/{membership_id}/permissions api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/notification-policies api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/notification-policies/{policy_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles/{role_id} api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles/{role_id}/permissions api path removed without deprecation
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/settings api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/api-keys/{api_key_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/memberships/{membership_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/notification-policies/{policy_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles/{role_id} api path removed without deprecation
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/settings api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/api-keys api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/api-keys/{api_key_id}/rotate api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/invitations api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/invitations/{invitation_id}/resend api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/leave api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/memberships api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/notification-policies api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/transfer-ownership api path removed without deprecation
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/logo api path removed without deprecation
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}/roles/{role_id}/permissions api path removed without deprecation
#     Added new (flat) paths:
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization api path added
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization/api-keys/{api_key_id} api path added
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization/invitations/{invitation_id} api path added
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization/logo api path added
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization/memberships/{membership_id} api path added
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization/notification-policies/{policy_id} api path added
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles/{role_id} api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/api-keys api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/api-keys/{api_key_id} api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/audit-logs api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/invitations api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/memberships api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/memberships/{membership_id} api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/memberships/{membership_id}/permissions api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/notification-policies api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/notification-policies/{policy_id} api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles/{role_id} api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles/{role_id}/permissions api path added
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/settings api path added
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization api path added
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization/api-keys/{api_key_id} api path added
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization/memberships/{membership_id} api path added
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization/notification-policies/{policy_id} api path added
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles/{role_id} api path added
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization/settings api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/api-keys api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/api-keys/{api_key_id}/rotate api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/invitations api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/invitations/{invitation_id}/resend api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/leave api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/memberships api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/notification-policies api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles api path added
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/transfer-ownership api path added
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organization/logo api path added
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organization/roles/{role_id}/permissions api path added
#     Permissive catch-alls (contiguous-substring match) covering any other finding
#     emitted for the old removed tenancy org-scoped paths or the new flat ones (param
#     pattern additions, idempotency-key header additions, success-status changes,
#     response-property removals). Path-scoped on purpose — they only match findings
#     naming these tenancy paths, so unrelated future breaking changes still fail the
#     gate. Both old placeholder spellings ({organization_id} and the underscore-free
#     {id} that older base specs emit) are covered by the literal path prefixes below
#     (no param token, so they match either form). The flat catch-alls use a trailing
#     slash (or PATCH/DELETE, which the plural account routes never use) so they never
#     match the account-level `…/tenancy/organizations` list/create/by-slug routes:
/api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{organization_id}
/api/v1/tenancy/organizations/{id}
api get /api/v1/tenancy/organization/
api post /api/v1/tenancy/organization/
api put /api/v1/tenancy/organization/
api patch /api/v1/tenancy/organization
api delete /api/v1/tenancy/organization

# Authenticated self-service MFA + passkey-registration routes moved under
# /auth/me/* (pre-first-release namespace cleanup: everything that acts on your
# own account lives under /me, matching auth-methods + sessions). The public
# login-flow routes (/auth/mfa/login, /auth/webauthn/authenticate/*) stayed put.
# New /auth/me/* paths are additive (non-breaking); only the old paths' removal
# is flagged. Pinned by auth + user + security integration tests.
api get /api/v1/auth/mfa api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/auth/mfa/enroll api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/auth/mfa/enroll/confirm api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/auth/mfa/verify api path removed without deprecation
api delete /api/v1/auth/mfa/{mfa_method_id} api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/auth/webauthn/register/options api path removed without deprecation
api post /api/v1/auth/webauthn/register/verify api path removed without deprecation
